metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` can actually consume code-review `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars (which use `blocking`/`advisory` buckets and `change_fingerprint`, not `findings` and `material_fingerprint`), or explicitly name a separate derivation script/notebook for the code-review corpus.
findings.finding: `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` reads plan-review-specific sidecar fields (`data.get('findings', [])` and `data.get('material_fingerprint')`) that are ABSENT from code-review sidecars (`code_review_result_v1`), which store findings in `blocking`/`advisory` buckets and use `change_fingerprint` instead. Running this script with `--impact-model-version code-v2` against the code-review corpus will silently yield zero findings and no revision-response signal, producing a vacuous calibration result — yet the plan names it as the 'mandated evidence gate before any threshold-down'.
findings.location: ## What (step 4): 'Run docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py + the version-segmented replay (docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version code-v2) per ADR 0036'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) note that a custom derivation script/notebook is needed (not `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`) and commit it under `docs/experiments/` as the AC1 artifact, or (b) extend `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` to handle the `code_review_result_v1` schema (merging `blocking`+`advisory` into its `findings` accumulator and using `change_fingerprint` as the revision key). The plan's AC1 should name whichever artifact will actually run.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the adjudication corpus assembly and the `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` replay correctly read the code-review sidecar schema (`blocking`/`advisory`/`coaching` buckets) rather than the plan-review `findings` key — or specify a separate extraction step that maps code-review sidecar payloads into the format the calibration tool expects.
findings.finding: The mandated `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version code-v2` replay will extract ZERO findings from code-review sidecars because the tool reads `data.get('findings', [])` (plan-review sidecar schema) while code-review `build_payload` writes findings into separate `blocking`/`advisory`/`coaching` keys — not a `findings` key. The ADR-0036 evidence gate will silently produce an empty corpus table rather than failing loudly, making the A/B + segmented-replay AC unverifiable.
findings.location: What section — 'Run docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py + the version-segmented replay (docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version code-v2)'; also AC line 3 ('The ADR-0036 A/B gate result … is recorded and passes.').
findings.suggested_fix: Add an extraction step (script or notebook) that reads `.tickets-tracker/*/*-REVIEW_RESULT.json` sidecars whose `data.schema == 'code_review_result_v1'` and `data.impact_model_version == 'code-v2'`, flattens `blocking + advisory` into a finding list (each carrying `criteria`, `impact`, `validity`, `priority`), and either (a) feeds that list directly to the precision-sweep computation or (b) rewrites it into the plan-review sidecar shape so `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` can consume it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version code-v2` produces meaningful output for code-review findings, or document which columns are valid for code-review (e.g., fire-rate / validity / priority percentiles) and which are structurally absent (rev_rr, material-fingerprint revision-response), and record this limitation alongside the ADR-0036 gate result in the ticket comment.
findings.finding: calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py was built for plan-review sidecars: its automated-proposal logic keys on plan-review criterion IDs (G6, COH, T5e, etc.) and its revision-response signal measures material_fingerprint deltas between consecutive plan-review rounds of the SAME ticket — a signal that does not exist for code-review, where revisions are Gerrit patch-sets, not ticket fingerprint changes. Pointing this script at code-review sidecars (--impact-model-version code-v2) will produce per-criterion fire-rate / validity rows, but (a) the criterion labels will be code-review criterion IDs the script has never been validated against, and (b) the revision-response column (rev_rr) will read ~0 for all criteria because code-review tickets do not go through repeated plan-review cycles with material_fingerprint changes. The plan treats this script's output as a sufficient ADR-0036 evidence gate without noting or bridging this structural mismatch.
findings.location: What section — 'Run docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py + the version-segmented replay (docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version code-v2) per ADR 0036'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the AC/What section acknowledging that rev_rr will be ~0 for code-review criteria (no plan-revision loop), and specify that the ADR-0036 evidence gate will rely on the fire-rate, validity, and priority-percentile columns only — explicitly excluding the revision-response column as inapplicable to code-review.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the calibration replay will extract findings from code-review sidecars (which store them under `blocking`/`advisory`, not `findings`) — either by updating the tool's loader, adding an adapter script, or documenting an explicit merge step before invoking `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`.
findings.finding: The plan mandates running `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version code-v2` over code-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecars, but the tool reads `data.get('findings', [])` while the code-review sidecar schema stores findings under `blocking`, `advisory`, and `coaching` keys — not `findings`. This is a proven schema mismatch: the tool will extract zero findings from every code-review sidecar, silently producing empty per-criterion tables.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add an explicit step: 'Pre-process code-v2 REVIEW_RESULT sidecars to normalise findings: merge `blocking + advisory` lists into a `findings`-keyed structure (with a `decision` field set accordingly) before passing to `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`, or extend the tool's `load()` function to handle both schemas.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` handles the code-review revision-response signal gap (rev_rr ≈ 0 for all code-review criteria): either extend the script to define 'revision' as Gerrit patch-set progression, or explicitly state that rev_rr is not used for the code-review calibration and only the precision-crossover table (from the new adjudication script) constitutes the ADR-0036 gate for this story.
findings.finding: calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py's revision-response signal (rev_rr) is structurally inapplicable to code-review sidecars: it measures material_fingerprint deltas between consecutive REVIEW_RESULT rounds of the SAME ticket — a plan-review-only concept. Code-review artifacts are keyed on Gerrit change IDs and do not accumulate fingerprint-changing re-review rounds per ticket. Running --impact-model-version code-v2 will silently yield rev_rr ≈ 0 for all criteria, and the script's BLOCK-ELIGIBLE branch gates on rev_rr >= 0.6 — so no code-review criterion will ever be classified as block-eligible by this tool. The plan presents this run as the mandated ADR-0036 evidence gate without noting or bridging this structural mismatch.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan note: 'The rev_rr column from calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py is not meaningful for code-review sidecars (no fingerprint-round accumulation); the ADR-0036 gate for this story relies solely on ab_impact_model.py (HIGH↔NIT separation) and the precision(block-worthy | impact >= t) table from the new derivation script. The calibrate script is run only for fire-rate and validity columns.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the Sonnet-scored adjudication protocol: specify the prompt schema (what question is asked, what input fields are included, what the allowed label vocabulary is), how the held-out human validation set is sampled, and what inter-rater agreement threshold is required before using Sonnet labels in the precision computation.
findings.finding: The plan references 'Sonnet-scored simulation with held-out adjudication acceptable for scale' as the adjudication methodology, but the protocol is never specified: no prompt schema, no definition of what the model is asked to decide, no vocabulary for the adjudication label (block-worthy vs not?), no inter-rater agreement threshold, and no description of how the held-out human adjudication set is constructed to validate the Sonnet labels. The decision rule used during adjudication is not co-located with the adjudication corpus schema (AC1).
findings.location: What section, bullet 2 ('Adjudicate each finding with impact >= 0.3 as block-worthy vs not (blind to its score where feasible), mirroring the b463 plan-review methodology (manual scoring of the would-block slice; Sonnet-scored simulation with held-out adjudication acceptable for scale)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC3 to describe the actual gate: 'The ADR-0036 A/B gate result (`separation > 0.30 AND median_nit < 0.30`, absolute — not relative to baseline) is recorded and the script exits 0.'
findings.finding: AC3 misdescribes the ADR-0036 A/B gate as 'HIGH<->NIT separation vs baseline', but `ab_impact_model.py` is explicit that the baseline score 'does NOT gate' and the actual gate is an ABSOLUTE criterion: `separation > 0.30 AND median_nit < 0.30`. The baseline ('old mean impact') is printed 'for transparency' only. An executor checking AC3 against the script output could incorrectly treat a baseline-beating result as a pass when the absolute criterion fails.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'The ADR-0036 A/B gate result (HIGH<->NIT separation vs baseline) is recorded and passes.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(HIGH<->NIT separation vs baseline)' with '(absolute: `median(HIGH)−median(NIT) > 0.30` AND `median(NIT) < 0.30`; baseline is reported for transparency but does NOT gate — see `ab_impact_model.py`)' in AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either confirm that a Sonnet-scored adjudication harness already exists (with a pointer to it), or remove the simulation path as an option and scope the adjudication as manual-only, or explicitly add 'build adjudication harness' as a scoped step.
findings.finding: The plan offers 'Sonnet-scored simulation with held-out adjudication acceptable for scale' as an adjudication path, but no such simulation harness exists in the repo. The plan-review b463 methodology it mirrors was based on manual scoring of plan-review sidecars — a different domain with human-readable finding text. For code-review findings, a 'Sonnet-scored simulation' would need to be built (prompting Sonnet to adjudicate whether each finding is block-worthy given its impact score and consequence binaries). The plan presents this as a known-viable option without identifying that the tooling does not exist and would need to be constructed.
findings.location: What section — 'Sonnet-scored simulation with held-out adjudication acceptable for scale'

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism for joining REVIEW_RESULT findings to Gerrit post-merge history (e.g., 'git log on the Gerrit-mirrored main branch filtered by change-id,' or 'manual lookup in the Gerrit web UI') and state any auth preconditions, OR narrow the corpus-assembly scope to ticket-store data only and define 'evidence' for the adjudication table accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts joining the findings corpus to 'Gerrit changes and post-merge history (follow-up fix commits, red-main incidents)' as part of corpus assembly, but names no API endpoint, CLI subcommand, script, or auth precondition for retrieving this external Gerrit history. The `GerritClient` class exists in `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` but is a runtime voter component, not a corpus-assembly or history-retrieval tool. No method for fetching 'follow-up fix commits' or 'red-main incidents' is specified.
findings.location: What section — 'joined to their Gerrit changes and post-merge history (follow-up fix commits, red-main incidents)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a default in the plan for when Sonnet simulation is permissible vs. required manual review — e.g. 'manual adjudication for all findings with impact ≥ 0.5 (estimated ≤ 20 items); Sonnet simulation acceptable for impact 0.3–0.49, with ≥ 10% held-out manual validation.'
findings.finding: The plan states that Sonnet-scored simulation is 'acceptable for scale' but does not define what scale triggers this shortcut or what fraction of items must still receive manual adjudication — leaving the executor to choose the methodology mix arbitrarily.
findings.location: What — adjudication step: 'Sonnet-scored simulation with held-out adjudication acceptable for scale'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'where feasible' with a stated default: e.g. 'blind review is required; if the tooling cannot hide the score, record each adjudication with the score visible and flag those rows in the corpus as non-blind for sensitivity analysis.'
findings.finding: 'Blind to its score where feasible' is vague — 'where feasible' defers the blinding decision to the executor without a default, making the adjudication methodology partially undefined.
findings.location: What — adjudication step: 'adjudicate each finding with impact >= 0.3 as block-worthy vs not (blind to its score where feasible)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Move the threshold recommendation and precision-crossover justification (including 0.54-band and 0.60-ceiling analyses) from a ticket comment into a durable committed artifact — either a README/summary file alongside the adjudication table in docs/experiments/, or as an ADR-0036 amendment — so the decision rationale is version-controlled alongside the corpus.
findings.finding: The threshold recommendation and its precision-crossover justification (including 0.54-band and 0.60-ceiling analyses) are written only to a ticket comment (AC 2), not to a durable, version-controlled artifact. Ticket comments are ephemeral and not recoverable if the ticket is archived or the tracker changes, leaving the rationale for the eventual config change effectively unrecoverable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, item 2: 'The chosen threshold and its precision-crossover justification are recorded in a comment on this ticket'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC requiring that docs/experiments/code_review_threshold_recommendation.md (or similar) be committed with: chosen threshold, precision table across candidate thresholds, 0.54-band analysis, 0.60-ceiling analysis, and link to adjudication corpus. The ticket comment can reference this file rather than contain the substance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving assertion to AC3: specify which metric the A/B gate must emit (e.g. Cohen's h or precision-recall AUC), its passing threshold (e.g. HIGH/NIT precision delta > 0.1 vs. baseline), and the command fragment that extracts and asserts it (e.g. `python docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py ... | jq -e '.high_nit_separation > 0.1'`).
findings.finding: AC3 states the A/B gate result 'passes' without defining what the gate's success looks like — no numeric threshold, no output assertion, no exit-code check — so an executor cannot determine whether the gate passed or failed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'The ADR-0036 A/B gate result (HIGH<->NIT separation vs baseline) is recorded and passes.'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to: 'The ADR-0036 A/B gate result is recorded: `python docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py --model code-v2` exits 0 AND emits HIGH<->NIT separation delta ≥ [baseline + X] (per ADR-0036 §4 success criterion); the raw output is committed alongside the adjudication table.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the What/AC1 whether the new derivation script should extend `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` (add adjudication-join + precision columns) or be a separate sibling script, and if the latter, specify that it imports/reuses the existing `load()` function rather than reimplementing corpus loading.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 calls for a 'derivation script or notebook' committed under docs/experiments/ to compute the adjudication table and precision-crossover analysis, while the existing docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py already automates a precision-first threshold proposal from the same REVIEW_RESULT corpus (same data source, overlapping threshold-selection logic). Without clarification, an executor may write a standalone script that reduplicate the corpus-loading and threshold-proposal mechanics already in calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py.
findings.location: AC1 / What section ('Compute precision(block-worthy | impact >= t)…') / docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, add: 'The derivation script SHOULD import or extend `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.load()` for corpus loading rather than reimplementing sidecar parsing; new logic is the adjudication join and per-threshold precision computation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name and spec the new derivation script or notebook (e.g. `docs/experiments/code_review_block_threshold_calibration.py`) that accepts the adjudicated corpus and outputs `precision(block-worthy | impact >= t)` for each candidate threshold — this is the missing edit target the plan currently leaves implicit.
findings.finding: The plan's core analytical computation — 'precision(block-worthy | impact >= t) across candidate thresholds t in {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.54, 0.6}' using an adjudicated block-worthy label — is not implemented by either named script. calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py computes fire-rate/validity/revision-response signals and proposes thresholds via a heuristic (rr >= 0.6 AND mv >= 0.55), with no per-threshold precision crossover against an adjudicated corpus. ab_impact_model.py computes HIGH↔NIT median separation on the fixture, also without a precision crossover. The plan names these two existing scripts as the mechanism but they cannot produce the required output without new code.
findings.location: What section: 'Compute precision(block-worthy | impact >= t)...' and AC1 ('committed...derivation script or notebook') and AC3 ('ADR-0036 A/B gate result...recorded and passes')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Implement docs/experiments/code_review_block_threshold_calibration.py that (a) loads the adjudicated corpus artifact, (b) for each threshold t in {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.54, 0.6} computes precision = |block-worthy ∩ impact>=t| / |impact>=t|, and (c) prints the crossover table. Update AC1 to name this script explicitly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the derivation script (e.g., `docs/experiments/calibrate_code_review_block_threshold.py`) and specify whether it imports/extends `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`'s `load()` function or stands alone, and confirm that the precision-crossover computation (adjudication join + per-threshold precision) is its responsibility rather than an extension of the existing scripts.
findings.finding: The plan's core deliverable — computing `precision(block-worthy | impact >= t)` across candidate thresholds {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.54, 0.6} over an adjudicated corpus — is absent from both named scripts. `ab_impact_model.py` computes HIGH↔NIT median separation on a pre-labeled fixture; `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` produces per-criterion fire-rate/validity/rev_rr rows. Neither script performs threshold-precision crossover analysis over a new adjudicated corpus joined to Gerrit history. The plan's AC1 calls for 'a derivation script or notebook' but does not name it, reconcile it with the existing scripts, or specify whether it extends or replaces them.
findings.location: What section — 'Compute precision(block-worthy | impact >= t) across candidate thresholds t in {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.54, 0.6}' / AC1

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
